acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** the GM selects a *Series Power Level* (typically PL 10) **THEN** the system establishes that PL as the cap for all *Player Characters* in the series **AND** the *Starting PP Budget* is derived as PL × 15 **BUT** individual heroes cannot negotiate a different PL for themselves **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_024.md` — "Power Level" section, lines 1650–1767
acceptance_criteria.text: **WHEN** the GM selects a *Series Power Level* (typically PL 10) **THEN** the system establishes that PL as the cap for all *Player Characters* in the series **AND** the *Starting PP Budget* is derived as PL × 15 **BUT** individual heroes cannot negotiate a different PL for themselves **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_024.md` — "Power Level" section, lines 1650–1767

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** the *Series* PL is set **THEN** every *Paired Trait Cap* defaults to 2 × PL (attack/effect, Dodge/Toughness, Parry/Toughness, Fortitude/Will) or PL+10 (skill modifier) **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_026.md` — PL limits section, lines 1808–1856

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** the GM sets PL to a non-default value (e.g., PL 8 or PL 12) **THEN** the system maps the chosen PL to the correct starting PP from the *Starting Power Points* table **AND** all five *Paired Trait Caps* recalculate for the new PL **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_025.md` — Basic Trait Costs table, lines 1768–1807; `chunk_026.md` — PL 8 and PL 12 descriptions

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** a player asks about *NPC Effective PL* **THEN** the GM derives the NPC's PL from their highest appropriate offensive/defensive pair **AND** no PP accounting is required for the NPC **BUT** the NPC is not constrained by the series *Paired Trait Caps* **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_027.md` — "Power Level & Non-player Characters", lines 1857–1902

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** no *Series Power Level* has been established **THEN** the system blocks all hero construction steps **AND** shows a prompt to the GM to set the series PL before design proceeds **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_024.md` — "Power Level" section
acceptance_criteria.text: **WHEN** no *Series Power Level* has been established **THEN** the system blocks all hero construction steps **AND** shows a prompt to the GM to set the series PL before design proceeds **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_024.md` — "Power Level" section

acceptance_criteria.text: **WHEN** the series PL is established **THEN** the system calculates *Starting PP* as PL × 15 **AND** presents the result as the hero's design budget **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_024.md` — "Starting Power Points" section, lines 1650–1767

acceptance_criteria.text: **WHEN** the *Player* assigns a rank to an ability (STR, STA, AGL, DEX, FGT, INT, AWE, or PRE) **THEN** the system charges *2 PP per Rank* **AND** deducts that cost from the *Remaining PP Budget* **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_025.md` — Basic Trait Costs table, lines 1768–1807

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** the *Player* reviews the eight abilities **THEN** the system displays each ability's current rank, its 2 PP/rank cost, and the total ability PP outlay **AND** shows the *Remaining PP Budget* after all ability allocations **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_025.md` — Basic Trait Costs table

stories.name: Spend Power Points on Defense Ranks

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** the *Player* purchases a defense rank (Dodge, Parry, Fortitude, or Will) **THEN** the system charges *1 PP per Rank* above the ability-provided base **AND** deducts the cost from the *Remaining PP Budget* **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_025.md` — Basic Trait Costs table, lines 1768–1807
acceptance_criteria.text: **WHEN** the *Player* purchases a defense rank (Dodge, Parry, Fortitude, or Will) **THEN** the system charges *1 PP per Rank* above the ability-provided base **AND** deducts the cost from the *Remaining PP Budget* **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_025.md` — Basic Trait Costs table, lines 1768–1807

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** the *Player* increases *Toughness* **THEN** the system simultaneously checks both the Dodge/*Toughness* pair and the Parry/*Toughness* pair against 2 × PL **AND** flags a *Defense Pair Cap* violation if either sum would exceed the ceiling **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_026.md` — Dodge & Toughness and Parry & Toughness limits

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** the *Player* sets Fortitude and Will **THEN** the system validates Fortitude+Will against 2 × PL **AND** flags the Fortitude/Will pair if the sum would exceed the cap **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_026.md` — "Fortitude & Will" limit

acceptance_criteria.name: **WHEN** all four active and resistance defenses are allocated **THEN** the system confirms each *Defense Pair Cap* is satisfied **AND** shows the headroom remaining in each pair for advancement planning **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_026.md` — PL limits section

acceptance_criteria.text: **WHEN** the *Player* selects a flat (non-ranked) advantage **THEN** the system charges 1 PP per advantage **AND** deducts the cost from the *Remaining PP Budget* **Evidence:** `HeroesHandbook-rules__chunk_025.md` — Basic Trait Costs table, lines 1768–1807
